tools.name: get_bank_rule
tools.description: Get full bank rule details including configuration (allocation percentages, accounts, tax settings).

tools.name: search_bank_rules
tools.description: Search bank rules.

tools.name: create_bank_rule
tools.description: Bank reconciliation rule — auto-matches bank records. Use directly on request; don't just list existing.

tools.name: update_bank_rule
tools.description: Update an existing bank reconciliation rule. FULL REPLACEMENT — the API replaces the entire rule on update. You MUST provide the complete configuration, appliesToReconciliationAccount, and name every time. Use get_bank_rule first to read the current state, merge your changes, then call this tool with the full payload. Dynamic strings: {{bankReference}}, {{bankPayee}}, {{bankDescription}}. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: delete_bank_rule
tools.description: Delete a bank reconciliation rule. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: view_auto_reconciliation
tools.description: Generate auto-reconciliation suggestions for a bank account. READ-ONLY — does NOT write. Returns execution-ready `suggestions[]`: each carries `recommendedTool` (the commit tool to call), `execute` (ready-to-pass args — merge bankAccountResourceId for reconcile_magic_match), `confidenceTier` (high/medium/low), and `autoCommitEligible` (true ⇒ safe to commit without review). Drive the flow: for each suggestion, if `autoCommitEligible` call its `recommendedTool` with `execute`; otherwise surface for confirmation — pass `includeReviewDetails:true` to also get `review[]` (the not-auto lines enriched with the bank line + the matched bill/invoice) for a side-by-side "does this match?" card. Pass `includeRaw:true` to also get the unmapped payload. On high-volume accounts the engine can 500 (OOM) → the tool returns `{degraded:true}`; recover via the `clio jobs bank-recon match` cascade matcher.

tools.name: list_fixed_assets
tools.description: List fixed assets. Returns name, reference, type, category, status, purchase amount, net book value.

tools.name: get_fixed_asset
tools.description: Get full fixed asset details including depreciation schedule, accounts, disposal info.

tools.name: search_fixed_assets
tools.description: Search fixed assets.

tools.name: create_fixed_asset
tools.description: Register a fixed asset linked to an existing purchase transaction (bill or journal).

tools.name: update_fixed_asset
tools.description: Update an existing fixed asset (draft or active). Can update name, depreciation settings, notes. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: delete_fixed_asset
tools.description: Delete a draft fixed asset. Cannot delete active/disposed assets. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: discard_fixed_asset
tools.description: Discard (write off) a fixed asset. Records the disposal and final depreciation. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: mark_fixed_asset_sold
tools.description: Mark a fixed asset as sold. Links to the sale transaction and records gain/loss. When the user asks to sell an asset, use this tool directly — retrieve the asset first with list_fixed_assets or search_fixed_assets to get the resourceId. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: transfer_fixed_asset
tools.description: Register a previously purchased fixed asset with accumulated depreciation.

tools.name: undo_fixed_asset_disposal
tools.description: Undo a fixed asset disposal (discard or sale). Restores the asset to active status. When the user asks to reverse or undo a disposal, use this tool directly. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: list_subscriptions
tools.description: List recurring subscriptions. Returns status, interval, next schedule date, amount, transaction type.

tools.name: get_subscription
tools.description: Get full subscription details including line items, schedule, and payment history.

tools.name: create_subscription
tools.description: Subscription — auto-generates invoices on schedule WITH proration. DIFFERENT from scheduled_invoice: subscriptions prorate partial periods (credit notes for mid-period changes), but currency/tax/account are immutable after creation. Use for software licenses, retainers, recurring SaaS. Invoices only (no bills).

tools.name: update_subscription
tools.description: Update an existing subscription (interval, end date, transaction template, or proratedConfig). When the user asks to update a subscription, use this tool directly. NOTE: accountResourceId and taxProfileResourceId on line items are immutable after creation — to change them, cancel and recreate. proratedConfig fields can be amended on update (proratedStartDate, proratedAdjustmentLineText, itemResourceId, includeNextPeriod). If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: delete_subscription
tools.description: Delete a subscription. Removes the recurring schedule. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: cancel_subscription
tools.description: Cancel an active subscription. Prorates remaining period and stops future billing. Safe to call — subscriptions can be reactivated after cancellation. Always use this tool when the user asks to cancel a subscription. Must cancel before delete. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: search_scheduled_transactions
tools.description: Search scheduled transactions (subscriptions / recurring invoices / journals / cash entries).

tools.name: generate_vat_ledger
tools.description: Generate VAT/GST ledger report showing all tax transactions for the period.

tools.name: generate_equity_movement
tools.description: Generate equity movement report (statement of changes in equity).

tools.name: generate_bank_balance_summary
tools.description: Generate bank balance summary report — snapshot of all bank account balances at a point in time.

tools.name: generate_bank_recon_summary
tools.description: Generate bank reconciliation summary for a specific bank account. Essential for month-end close — call this for each in-scope bank account for the current entity and reporting period. Use for bank recon status, unreconciled items count, or reconciliation summaries.

tools.name: generate_bank_recon_details
tools.description: Generate detailed bank reconciliation report showing matched/unmatched items.

tools.name: generate_fa_summary
tools.description: Generate fixed assets summary report grouped by account, type, category, or status.

tools.name: generate_fa_recon_summary
tools.description: Generate fixed assets reconciliation summary — reconciles asset register with general ledger.

tools.name: generate_ar_report
tools.description: Generate accounts receivable aging report. Shows outstanding invoices grouped by aging buckets. Use this when discussing customer payment allocation, credit control, or AR status — generate the report to show the full picture.

tools.name: get_ledger_highlights
tools.description: Get ledger highlights — summary metadata about the org general ledger: transaction counts by type, date range, active accounts, active currencies, and cross-currency detection. No parameters needed.

tools.name: list_contact_groups
tools.description: List contact groups with their associated contacts. Used to segment customers/suppliers.

tools.name: get_contact_group
tools.description: Get contact group details including all associated contacts.

tools.name: search_contact_groups
tools.description: Search contact groups.

tools.name: create_contact_group
tools.description: Create a new contact group for segmenting contacts (e.g., "VIP Customers", "Government", "Overdue > 90 Days"). If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: update_contact_group
tools.description: Update a contact group name or members. NOTE: Update endpoint has a known 500 bug on some orgs — retry or recreate if it fails. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: delete_contact_group
tools.description: Delete a contact group. Does not delete the contacts themselves. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: list_custom_fields
tools.description: List custom fields defined for the organization. Custom fields add extra data to invoices, bills, credit notes, and payments.

tools.name: get_custom_field
tools.description: Get a custom field by resourceId. Returns full definition including appliesTo flags and list options.

tools.name: search_custom_fields
tools.description: Search custom fields.

tools.name: create_custom_field
tools.description: Create a new custom field. Custom fields appear on transactions (invoices, bills, credit notes, payments).

tools.name: update_custom_field
tools.description: Update a custom field definition. Can change name, description, printOnDocuments, and appliesTo flags. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: delete_custom_field
tools.description: Delete a custom field. Removes it from all future transactions. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: list_withholding_tax_codes
tools.description: List withholding tax codes (industry classification codes for PH/SG compliance). Returns full list (no pagination).

tools.name: search_tax_profiles
tools.description: Search tax profiles. Use appliesToSale / appliesToPurchase filters to scope by transaction type.

tools.name: get_tax_profile
tools.description: Get full tax profile details including tax rate, type code, and status.

tools.name: update_tax_profile
tools.description: Update an existing tax profile (name, rate, or status). If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: create_claim_type
tools.description: Create a claim type (expense category for employee claims). Dedups by name. expenseAccountResourceId is server-required. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: update_claim_type
tools.description: Update a claim type. Omitted fields unchanged; clearFields NULLs optional fields. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: search_claim_types
tools.description: Search claim types.

tools.name: list_claim_types
tools.description: List claim types (alphabetical).

tools.name: get_claim_type
tools.description: Get a claim type incl. nested expense account + tax profile.

tools.name: delete_claim_type
tools.description: Soft-delete a claim type (name becomes reusable). If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: create_claim_profile
tools.description: Create a claim profile (per-employee policy bundle: approver, limits, visible types, balance account). Dedups by name. taxMode EXCLUSIVE is rejected. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: update_claim_profile
tools.description: Update a claim profile. Omitted fields unchanged; clearFields NULLs optional fields. Balance account cannot change while an employee has a non-zero balance. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: search_claim_profiles
tools.description: Search claim profiles.

tools.name: list_claim_profiles
tools.description: List claim profiles (alphabetical).

tools.name: get_claim_profile
tools.description: Get a claim profile incl. nested balance account.

tools.name: delete_claim_profile
tools.description: Soft-delete a claim profile. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: create_posting_rule
tools.description: Create a posting rule (how approved claims group into journals at conversion). outerAxis + innerAxis + lineDescriptionTemplate required. Dedups by name. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: update_posting_rule
tools.description: Update a posting rule. Omitted fields unchanged. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: search_posting_rules
tools.description: Search posting rules.

tools.name: list_posting_rules
tools.description: List posting rules (alphabetical).

tools.name: get_posting_rule
tools.description: Get a posting rule.

tools.name: delete_posting_rule
tools.description: Delete a posting rule. The org default cannot be deleted while default (set another default first). If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: search_claims
tools.description: Search/list employee-expense claims. Status: DRAFT|SUBMITTED|APPROVED|CONVERTED|REJECTED|CANCELLED (CONVERTED = "Processed"). Claims are born via create_claim, attachment, or conversion (no bare list — use search).

tools.name: get_claim
tools.description: Get a claim incl. line items, employee, amounts, status.

tools.name: create_claim
tools.description: Create an employee-expense claim directly (bare create, no attachment). Vendor is EITHER contactResourceId OR vendorName, never both. saveAsDraft defaults to true (lands DRAFT with relaxed line validation); set false to validate + submit in one call (lands SUBMITTED). reference is REQUIRED when submitting (saveAsDraft=false) — omitting it returns 422 CLAIM_REFERENCE_REQUIRED_AT_SUBMIT; a DRAFT saves without one. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: update_claim
tools.description: Update a DRAFT claim (partial — omitted fields unchanged). claimItems non-empty REPLACES all lines, [] clears. Set saveAsDraft=false to validate + submit in one call — reference is REQUIRED at submit (else 422 CLAIM_REFERENCE_REQUIRED_AT_SUBMIT); a DRAFT save needs none. Only DRAFT claims are editable. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: delete_claim
tools.description: Delete a claim. Only DRAFT / REJECTED / CANCELLED claims are deletable (else 422). If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: delete_claim_attachment
tools.description: Remove one attachment (receipt) from a claim. Returns the updated claim. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: submit_claim
tools.description: Submit a DRAFT claim for approval (DRAFT → SUBMITTED). Server enforces the transition. The claim must carry a reference — a referenceless DRAFT returns 422 CLAIM_REFERENCE_REQUIRED_AT_SUBMIT (set it via update_claim first). If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: approve_claim
tools.description: Approve a SUBMITTED claim (SUBMITTED → APPROVED). Approved claims become convertible to journals. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: reject_claim
tools.description: Reject a SUBMITTED/APPROVED claim with a reason (→ REJECTED). If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: cancel_claim
tools.description: Cancel a DRAFT/SUBMITTED claim with a reason (→ CANCELLED). If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: unpost_claim
tools.description: Unpost a CONVERTED claim — reverses the journal conversion back to APPROVED. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: bulk_submit_claims
tools.description: Bulk-submit DRAFT claims (ASYNC). Returns a job handle {jobId,...}; poll search_background_jobs by jobId for per-item results. Max 500 ids. Each claim must have a reference — referenceless claims fail per-item with CLAIM_REFERENCE_REQUIRED_AT_SUBMIT (check the job result). If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: bulk_approve_claims
tools.description: Bulk-approve SUBMITTED claims (ASYNC). Returns a job handle; poll search_background_jobs by jobId. Max 500 ids. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: bulk_reject_claims
tools.description: Bulk-reject claims with one shared reason (ASYNC). Returns a job handle; poll search_background_jobs by jobId. Max 500 ids. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: bulk_cancel_claims
tools.description: Bulk-cancel claims with one shared reason (ASYNC). Returns a job handle; poll search_background_jobs by jobId. Max 500 ids. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: bulk_delete_claims
tools.description: Bulk-delete claims (ASYNC; only DRAFT/REJECTED/CANCELLED are deletable). Returns a job handle; poll search_background_jobs by jobId. Max 500 ids. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: get_claim_tracking_tags
tools.description: List the tracking-tag values available across claims (picker; returns a bare array).

tools.name: get_claim_custom_field_values
tools.description: List the custom-field values available across claims (picker; returns a bare array).

tools.name: preview_claims_conversion
tools.description: Preview the journals an APPROVED claim batch would produce at conversion — no write. Returns expenseSubTotal, previewJournals, and reimbursement DCE counts; non-APPROVED claims come back under erroredClaims (check it is empty before converting). Needs a default posting rule or an explicit postingRuleResourceId.

tools.name: convert_claims_to_journal
tools.description: Convert an APPROVED claim batch into journals (atomic write). valueDate (YYYY-MM-DD) required. Preview first and confirm erroredClaims is empty. idempotencyKey is auto-generated for safe retries (pass your own to dedup across calls). includeReimbursementPayout adds a books-only reimbursement DCE — real-money disburse is intentionally not exposed. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: record_employee_payout
tools.description: Record a books-only employee payout (reimbursement or advance) — a DCE, no real-money transfer. amount, paymentAccountResourceId and valueDate are required; reference auto-generated if omitted. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: search_employee_payouts
tools.description: Search recorded employee payouts (reimbursements + advances).

tools.name: add_employee
tools.description: Create an employee (expense-claim member). The server requires userResourceId (a login user to bind — binding is PERMANENT) and claimProfileResourceId (REQUIRED — carries the employee balance account; the org default is NOT auto-applied). Dedups by email. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: update_employee
tools.description: Update an employee (partial; omit = no change). To ARCHIVE, set active=false (reversible — prefer over delete_employee). userResourceId is NOT editable here — use bind_employee_user. Set clearEmploymentType to unset the classification. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: search_employees
tools.description: Search employees by name, email, phone, manager, claim profile, active, currency.

tools.name: search_employee_balances
tools.description: Search the employee-balance directory (per-currency reimbursement balances owed).

tools.name: get_employee
tools.description: Get an employee incl. claim profile + user binding.

tools.name: delete_employee
tools.description: Delete an employee (server validates they are settled). Prefer update_employee active=false to archive reversibly. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: bind_employee_user
tools.description: Bind a login user to an OFFLINE employee (one with no user bound yet — e.g. created via import without a binding email). ONE-WAY and PERMANENT: allowed only while unbound; rebind is blocked once set. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: preprocess_employees_file
tools.description: Preprocess an uploaded employee import sheet (CSV/XLSX) — returns a parsed row preview to review before import_employees. Provide the sheet fileUrl.

tools.name: import_employees
tools.description: Bulk create/update/delete employees from resolved rows (max 100 per array). Server validates rows synchronously (422 with row errors) then queues an async job — poll search_background_jobs by jobId. Create rows need a bound user + claim profile. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: list_inventory_items
tools.description: List inventory-tracked items with stock details, COGS accounts, and purchase/sale accounts.

tools.name: get_inventory_balance
tools.description: Get current inventory balance for a specific item — quantity on hand, average cost, base unit.

tools.name: create_inventory_item
tools.description: Create a new inventory-tracked item. Requires itemCode, name, costingMethod (FIXED or WAC), cogsResourceId, and account links. Use --input for full JSON body. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: delete_contact
tools.description: Delete a contact. Cannot delete contacts with existing transactions. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: delete_account
tools.description: Delete a chart of accounts entry. Cannot delete accounts with existing transactions. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: get_cash_transfer
tools.description: Get full cash transfer details by resourceId.

tools.name: download_credit_note_pdf
tools.description: Download a customer credit note as PDF. Returns a file URL.

tools.name: get_transaction_summary
tools.description: Get a complete summary of any transaction — fetches the transaction, its attachments, and payment history in one call.

tools.name: bulk_finalize_drafts
tools.description: Finalize multiple draft transactions in one call. Attempts finalization for each item and returns per-item pass/fail results. Supports invoices, bills, and credit notes. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: bulk_invite_org_users
tools.description: Invite multiple users to the organization in one call. Accepts an array of user objects. Each user follows the same contract as invite_org_user. Returns per-user pass/fail results. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: bulk_create_contacts
tools.description: Create multiple contacts in one call. Deduplicates against existing contacts (exact name match).

tools.name: quick_fix_transactions
tools.description: Bulk-update transactions of the same type. Only included fields change; omitted fields preserved.

tools.name: quick_fix_line_items
tools.description: Bulk-update line items across transactions. Only included fields change.

tools.name: list_nano_classifiers
tools.description: List nano classifiers (tracking categories). Paginated. Nano classifiers tag line items with structured categories.

tools.name: get_nano_classifier
tools.description: Get a nano classifier by resourceId. Returns type and all classes.

tools.name: search_nano_classifiers
tools.description: Search nano classifiers (tracking categories).

tools.name: create_nano_classifier
tools.description: Create a nano classifier (tracking category). Provide a type name and list of class names. printable defaults to true. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: update_nano_classifier
tools.description: Update a nano classifier — change type name, modify classes, or toggle printable. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: delete_nano_classifier
tools.description: Delete a nano classifier. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: list_invoice_payments
tools.description: List all payments recorded against an invoice. Returns payment records with amounts, dates, and methods.

tools.name: list_invoice_credits
tools.description: List all credit notes applied to an invoice.

tools.name: reverse_invoice_credit
tools.description: Reverse (unapply) a credit note from an invoice. The credit note becomes UNAPPLIED again. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: list_bill_payments
tools.description: List all payments recorded against a bill. Returns payment records with amounts, dates, and methods.

tools.name: list_bill_credits
tools.description: List all credit notes applied to a bill.

tools.name: reverse_bill_credit
tools.description: Reverse (unapply) a supplier credit note from a bill. The credit note becomes UNAPPLIED again. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: get_scheduled_invoice
tools.description: Get a scheduled (recurring) invoice by resourceId.

tools.name: update_scheduled_invoice
tools.description: Update a scheduled invoice — change schedule settings and/or the invoice template. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: delete_scheduled_invoice
tools.description: Delete a scheduled (recurring) invoice. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: get_scheduled_bill
tools.description: Get a scheduled (recurring) bill by resourceId.

tools.name: update_scheduled_bill
tools.description: Update a scheduled bill — change schedule settings and/or the bill template. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: delete_scheduled_bill
tools.description: Delete a scheduled (recurring) bill. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: get_scheduled_journal
tools.description: Get a scheduled (recurring) journal by resourceId.

tools.name: update_scheduled_journal
tools.description: Update a scheduled journal — change schedule settings and/or the journal template. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: delete_scheduled_journal
tools.description: Delete a scheduled (recurring) journal. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: get_payment
tools.description: Get a specific payment record by resourceId. Returns payment amount, method, date, and reference.

tools.name: update_payment
tools.description: Update a payment record — correct amount, reference, date, method, or account. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: delete_payment
tools.description: Delete (void) a payment record. The associated invoice/bill balance is restored. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: get_export_columns
tools.description: List all exportable columns for an entity type, including their paths and data types. Use paths from this response to build custom column sets for export_records or preview_export_records.

tools.name: preview_export_records
tools.description: Preview an export before generating the file. Returns totalRecords, up to 10 sample rows, resolved column definitions, and a human-readable filterDescription. Use this to confirm scope before calling export_records. Pass either query OR filter, never both.

tools.name: export_records
tools.description: Raw entity-row XLSX export for one entity type (invoices/bills/contacts/items/journals/bank records/cashflow/fixed assets — one row per record, with column selection). Returns pre-signed download URL (~5 min), fileName, totalCount. Pass query OR filter, never both. Use preview_export_records first to confirm scope.

tools.name: search_background_jobs
tools.description: Search and poll background jobs. ANY operation that returns a jobId (bulk_upsert_contacts, bulk_upsert_items, bank statement import, magic file processing, etc.) can be tracked here. Filter by resourceId (the jobId value) to poll a specific job. Poll until status is SUCCESS, FAILED, or PARTIAL_SUCCESS. Use processedCount/failedCount/totalRecords for progress. IMPORTANT: filter by resourceId field, NOT jobId — they differ.

tools.name: bulk_upsert_contacts
tools.description: Bulk create/update contacts (max 500). resourceId per row → update (partial; omitted fields preserve existing). Omit resourceId → create. Async; returns jobId → poll search_background_jobs by resourceId; PARTIAL_SUCCESS data[0].errorDetails. Per-row failures in errorDetails (NOT failedRows — that's the sync pattern). On PARTIAL_SUCCESS: succeeded rows are committed; re-submit only failed rowIndex entries.

tools.name: bulk_upsert_invoices
tools.description: Max 500 invoices per call. FLAT shape — ONE line per invoice via itemDescription + totalAmount + invoiceAccountResourceId at row level. For multi-line, use bulk_upsert_invoice_line_items (nested lineItems[]).

tools.name: bulk_upsert_invoice_line_items
tools.description: Nested line items per invoice (max 500 invoices per call). Each row carries lineItems[] under its parent invoice (scoped by invoiceReference). Async; returns jobId → poll search_background_jobs by resourceId; PARTIAL_SUCCESS data[0].errorDetails. Dates are ISO 8601 only. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: bulk_upsert_bills
tools.description: Max 500 bills per call. FLAT shape: ONE line per bill via `itemDescription` + `totalAmount` + `billAccountResourceId` at row level. For multi-line bills use `bulk_upsert_bill_line_items` instead — that variant takes nested `lineItems[]`. Natural key: `billReference` (rows sharing one within a batch are MERGED — last wins). Async; returns jobId → poll search_background_jobs by resourceId; PARTIAL_SUCCESS data[0].errorDetails. Dates are ISO 8601 only — dateFormat field was removed. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: bulk_upsert_bill_line_items
tools.description: Nested line items per bill (max 500 bills per call). Each row carries lineItems[] under its parent bill (scoped by billReference). Async; returns jobId → poll search_background_jobs by resourceId; PARTIAL_SUCCESS data[0].errorDetails. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: bulk_upsert_customer_credit_notes
tools.description: Max 500 customer credit notes per call. Natural key: creditNoteReference. Async; returns jobId → poll search_background_jobs by resourceId; PARTIAL_SUCCESS data[0].errorDetails. Dates are ISO 8601 only. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: bulk_upsert_supplier_credit_notes
tools.description: Max 500 supplier credit notes per call. Natural key: creditNoteReference. Async; returns jobId → poll search_background_jobs by resourceId; PARTIAL_SUCCESS data[0].errorDetails. Dates are ISO 8601 only. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: bulk_upsert_journals
tools.description: Max 500 manual journals per call. NATURAL KEY: `journalReference` (NOT `reference` — every other bulk-upsert uses entityReference, this one is asymmetric). LEGS field: `journalEntries[]` (NOT `entries[]` — different from `clio journals create` which uses entries). Each leg has `accountResourceId` + `debitAmount` + `creditAmount` (debit + credit must balance per row). Async; returns jobId → poll search_background_jobs by resourceId; PARTIAL_SUCCESS data[0].errorDetails. Dates are ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD) only — dateFormat field was removed. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: bulk_upsert_fixed_assets
tools.description: Bulk create/update fixed assets (max 500). Natural key: reference.

tools.name: quick_reconcile
tools.description: Bulk-reconcile bank statement entries against a list of journals (max 500). Reconciliation mode is hardcoded to QUICK_RECON. Async; returns jobId → poll search_background_jobs by resourceId; PARTIAL_SUCCESS data[0].errorDetails. Caller provides bankAccountResourceId + journalsForReconciliation[] (each row: bankStatementEntryResourceId, journalReference, journalAccountResourceId). If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: apply_bank_rule
tools.description: Apply a pre-configured bank rule (action shortcut) to a batch of bank statement entries (max 500). Async — returns a jobId. Poll search_background_jobs filtered by resourceId. The rule executes its configured action (e.g. RECONCILE_WITH_DIRECT_CASH_ENTRY) on each entry. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: reconcile_direct_cash_entry
tools.description: Reconcile a bank statement entry with a single cash-in / cash-out line. Direction (cash-in vs cash-out) is INFERRED from the bank entry sign. Sync — returns the reconciled entry status. NOT idempotent: calling twice on the same bankStatementEntryResourceId creates duplicate journals. Confirm reconciled-state via view_auto_reconciliation before retrying. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: reconcile_cash_journal
tools.description: Reconcile a bank statement entry with a multi-line cashflow journal (multiple cash splits, max 200 lines). Sync — returns the reconciled entry status. NOT idempotent — confirm reconciled-state before retrying. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: reconcile_manual_journal
tools.description: ⚠️ CALLER PROVIDES ONLY THE OFFSET LEG(S) — backend AUTO-ADDS the bank-side leg from the statement entry. Sending both debit AND credit legs → 422 "sum of debit and credit amounts are not equal" (your sides duplicate after the auto-add). Reconcile a bank statement entry with a double-entry manual journal. Sync — returns the reconciled entry status. valueDate prefills from bank entry if omitted. NOT idempotent. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: reconcile_cash_transfer
tools.description: Reconcile a bank statement entry with an inter-account transfer. Sync — returns the reconciled entry status. amount is required only when the counterparty account is in a non-functional currency. NOT idempotent. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: reconcile_invoice_receipt
tools.description: CREATES A NEW invoice and auto-reconciles it to a bank statement entry (AR). To match an EXISTING open invoice instead, use reconcile_with_payments (do not create a duplicate). ⚠️ BSE must have `paymentDirection: PAYIN` (money in — AR). Produce via `bank add-records` with a POSITIVE amount (creates `credit_amount > 0` → PAYIN). Statement-imported BSEs from `bank import` also work. The error code "Invalid business transaction type" (422) is misleadingly named — the actual gate is direction, not entry type. Sync — returns the reconciled entry status. valueDate / dueDate / payment amount prefill from the bank entry if omitted. NOT idempotent. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: reconcile_bill_receipt
tools.description: CREATES A NEW bill and auto-reconciles it to a bank statement entry (AP). To match an EXISTING open bill instead, use reconcile_with_payments (do not create a duplicate). ⚠️ BSE must have `paymentDirection: PAYOUT` (money out — AP). Produce via `bank add-records` with a NEGATIVE amount (creates `debit_amount > 0` → PAYOUT). Statement-imported BSEs from `bank import` also work. The error code "Invalid business transaction type" (422) is misleadingly named — the actual gate is direction, not entry type. Sync — returns the reconciled entry status. valueDate / dueDate / payment amount prefill from the bank entry if omitted. NOT idempotent. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: reconcile_with_payments
tools.description: USE THIS to match a bank statement entry to an EXISTING open bill / invoice / payment (NOT reconcile_bill_receipt or reconcile_invoice_receipt, which CREATE a new bill/invoice). Pass the open transaction's cashflowTransactionResourceId (from search_cashflow_transactions or a view_auto_reconciliation suggestion) in businessTransactionPayments[] — this CREATES the payment AND reconciles in one call; no pay_bill / pay_invoice first. FX is resolved server-side — pass NO rate for the common case (only supply paymentAmount + currencySettings for the rare bill-currency ≠ bank-currency case). Sync — returns the reconciled entry status. NOT idempotent: a blind retry double-creates a payment; confirm reconciled-state via search_bank_records(status: RECONCILED) before retrying. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: reconcile_magic_match
tools.description: Bulk-accept MAGIC_MATCH suggestions: reconcile many bank entries against EXISTING open business transactions and/or payments in one call (max 500 entries). Feed entries from view_auto_reconciliation suggestions. Returns a per-entry split { reconciled[], failed[] } — a 200 with a non-empty failed[] is a PARTIAL success; surface failed entries (with their errorCode) and loop. Entry-level idempotency-keyed server-side. Use reconcile_with_payments for a single precise match; use this to accept a batch of suggestions at once. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: reconcile_learned_prediction
tools.description: Accept an ML learned-prediction suggestion to reconcile a bank entry. predictedPayload + predictedPayloadSchemaVersion are passed through VERBATIM from a view_auto_reconciliation suggestion (recommendationType MAGIC_RECONCILE_WITH_CASH_IN_OUT) — do not construct them by hand. Pass retryToken to force a fresh journal on an edit-then-retry; omit it for an idempotent replay. Sync — returns the reconciled entry status. On failure (stale/invalid payload), fall back to reconcile_with_payments — do NOT retry the same opaque payload. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: validate_drafts
tools.description: BULK validate up to 500 draft business transactions in ONE call, mixing any btType. SYNC — returns per-item errors + display data; no state change. Run before convert_drafts_to_active.

tools.name: convert_drafts_to_active
tools.description: BULK promote — finalize up to 500 draft business transactions to ACTIVE in ONE call, mixing any combination of invoices, bills, and credit notes (no per-type tools needed). Async; returns jobId → poll search_background_jobs by resourceId; PARTIAL_SUCCESS data[0].errorDetails. NOT idempotent: a second call on already-promoted drafts returns 422. Filter the draft list by status: DRAFT before submitting. Pair with validate_drafts for a pre-flight check on large batches. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: submit_drafts_for_approval
tools.description: BULK submit — route up to 500 draft business transactions into the approval workflow in ONE call, mixing any combination of invoices, bills, and credit notes. Async; returns jobId → poll search_background_jobs by resourceId; PARTIAL_SUCCESS data[0].errorDetails. NOT idempotent on drafts that already have an in-flight approval request. If this action involved a judgment call, record it (jot field or jot tool).

tools.name: search_help_center
tools.description: Search the Jaz help center for how-to articles, feature guides, and accounting concepts. Use this for "how do I..." or "what is..." questions about Jaz: bank reconciliation, GST/VAT filing, multi-currency, fixed assets, custom fields, integrations, and so on. Returns top matching articles with title, section, content, and source URL.

tools.name: jot
tools.description: Record judgment entries in the org's judgment journal. THE RULE: log a judgment when you chose among real alternatives and a write followed, or when you deliberately decided NOT to write; skip mechanical actions and calls where the platform, the data, or the user left you no choice. Jot AFTER the write succeeds, never before, and carry the written record's resourceId in refs. Kinds: CLASSIFICATION account/tax/category choice · MATCH decided which records correspond · SCOPE chose what is in or out of a set · ASSUMPTION filled a gap the data left open · RISK proceeded despite a known concern · METHOD picked a calculation or approach · RECOVERY decided how to proceed after a failure (incl. holding a write) · DEVIATION knowingly departed from a stated rule. Accepting or rejecting an inbound external document (an e-invoice a counterparty sent) is a FINALIZE act, so pin it. A decided omission (you deliberately did not post something) is RECOVERY, which pins via withheld-write, not DEVIATION. Always give why: a call without a basis is half a record. Batch related entries in one call; each entry is acked independently. An ack with duplicateCount > 0 means this judgment is already recorded: do not re-jot identical calls; recall first on repeated workflows. Style: tight, factual, plain punctuation; never repeat content across fields (refs carry the record, why carries the basis); detail belongs only in why/ruledOut/frame where it earns its place.

tools.name: recall
tools.description: Search the org's judgment journal (jots) for precedents, prior judgment calls with their basis. Check BEFORE repeating a judgment on the same entity, kind of decision, or workflow: a prior call with its why beats re-deriving from scratch; a FLAG or REJECT disposition on it is a warning, ENDORSE a strong precedent. Sort is pinned server-side (CRITICAL and withheld-write RECOVERY entries first, then newest). An empty page means no prior judgments match this filter, or none are recorded for this org yet.

tools.name: present_questions
tools.description: Present the user with a bounded multiple-choice question (or a short sequence of them) as an interactive pick-list, instead of asking in prose. Use as your FINAL action when the user must choose among a KNOWN, finite set of options you ALREADY have from context (a prior search/list result, org defaults, or your own knowledge) to continue (e.g. "which bank account?", "which of these duplicate bills?"). Give 2-4 options per question — curate to the most likely, never dump a long list — and at most 4 questions; set multiSelect per question when more than one may apply. List the options and stop — the user's pick arrives as their next message and starts a new turn. Ask several questions at once when more than one thing is unspecified. Prose-ask only when there's no bounded set to offer (a free-text name or amount), to confirm destructive actions, or instead of fetching data just to populate options. Terminal: do not call further tools or re-list the options in text afterward.

tools.name: navigate
tools.description: Dashboard navigation: discover destinations, then build deep links. Without `destination`: lists valid destinations as { key, label, kind } — filter with `query` (substring over key + label), `resource` (e.g. "sales"), or `kind` ("screen" | "modal"); results are capped, narrow if `truncated`. With `destination`: builds the URL to hand the user — a screen (e.g. "reports.profit-and-loss") or a record modal (e.g. "sales.modal.view-sale"); pass `resourceId` only for a record-specific modal (view/edit one record), never for screens or create/"new" modals. Returns { url, kind, label }; the link opens in the user's current org. Never write dashboard URLs or destination keys from memory — call with no destination to discover keys first.
